description: Christchurch mosque shootings (March 15, 2019) killed 51 people, triggering near-immediate legislative action. Support for strict gun reform surged from ~55% to 85%+. NZ Parliament passed Arms Amendment Act in 26 days with near-unanimous vote (119-1). Tests crisis-driven consensus formation and rapid policy response.

key_events.description: March 15, 2019: Christchurch mosque shootings kill 51 people. PM Ardern immediately signals gun reform: 'Our gun laws will change.'
Government announces ban on military-style semi-automatic weapons within 6 days. National (opposition) leader supports. Overwhelming bipartisan consensus.
Arms Amendment Act passes Parliament 119-1 on April 10 (26 days after attack). Gun buyback program announced.
Buyback program begins. Over 50,000 firearms surrendered. International praise for NZ's swift response. Ardern's leadership widely lauded.
Second tranche of reforms passed (Arms Legislation Act) creating national firearms register. Minor opposition from rural hunting community but support holds at 85%+.

calibration_notes: Tests extreme consensus formation after mass casualty event. Key patterns: largest single-event opinion shift in calibration set (+30pts in days), facilitated by NZ's smaller population, less entrenched gun culture vs US, strong PM leadership, bipartisan elite consensus eliminating counter-cues, social cohesion response to attack on minority community, no significant organized opposition (no NRA equivalent). Contrast with US post-shooting patterns where support surges then fades. Colmar Brunton, Reid Research polling data used.
